title: /kernel
summary_text: Yes, a non-healthy/“blocked” bootstrap state is representable without breaking local tooling, but not as a first-class `receipt.mode`. - `kernel-bootstrap-receipt` accepts any `mode` string and persists it verbatim (no enum validation): [scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh#L49), [scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh#L88). - `kernel-runtime-health` only treats `degraded-allowed` specially; all other `mode` values flow through the normal branch and can still become `healthy` if evidence exists: [scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh#L202). - “Blocked” is derived from health `state` (`invalid` etc.), not from `receipt.mode`; lifecycle maps non-healthy states to `blocked`: [scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh#L49). - Downstream tooling remains compatible: 4-pane maps non-`healthy`/non-`degraded-allowed` to `BLOCKED`: [scripts/lib/kernel-4pane-surface.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-4pane-surface.sh#L91). - Supporting checks confirm this behavior (invalid health returns non-zero, degraded path enforced): [tests/test-kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-runtime-health.sh#L122), [tests/test-kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-runtime-health.sh#L106). Practical result from isolated smoke run: `mode=blocked` was accepted, and health became `healthy` when provider evidence was present; without evidence it became `invalid` with lifecycle `blocked`.
